name: Todd Russell
title: Mechanical Engineer
tagline: Multidisciplinary mechanical, mechatronics, and process engineer solving complex problems across automation, machine vision, and precision manufacturing, from R&D through production.

experience.intro: Started as Engineer II after internship, then promoted to Engineer III within a year.

groups.name: Module Development & Efficiency
bullets: Drove a 35% relative improvement in initial module efficiency and a 35× improvement in module degradation lifespan as lead of module development, owning all loss mechanisms related to modulization of the solar cell.
Achieved a world-record 24.0% efficiency perovskite module through joint development with NLR (formerly NREL).
Established baseline production of scalable module processes.

groups.name: Laser Scribing Automation
bullets: Increased laser scribing throughput by 500% and yield by 5%, decreased takt time by 33%, and improved 6σ positional repeatability from 60 μm to 20 μm.
Automated the laser scribing process, transitioning operation from engineer-level to technician-level.
Streamlined the process and wrote a parameterized codebase enabling a recipe-based UI capable of adapting to evolving production needs.
Developed and built machine vision system to automatically adjust for position and skew of solar cell relative to nominal.

groups.name: Defect Tracking
bullets: Identified two defect types present on 50% of modules causing a 20× greater impact on degradation performance than all other defect categories.
Developed a defect Pareto linking defect frequency and severity to degradation performance.
Spearheaded defect tracking initiative and authored the functional specification for the software team, enabling company-wide rollout and large-dataset-driven process improvements.

groups.name: DMC Tray Scanner
bullets: Designed and built a linear Data Matrix Code (DMC) scanning system that captures each solar cell's tray position in a single scanner pass.
Enabled tray-position tracking across 3 manufacturing processes, supporting positional uniformity analysis and linking solar cell location during a process to its performance data.
Reduced solar cell scan time by ~90% compared to prior single-solar-cell scan method.

groups.name: Large Solar Cell Photobooth
bullets: Converted a large-area solar cell photoluminescence characterization box into a multi-mode photobooth, enabling visual characterization imaging of 400mm solar cells at production scale.
Engineered and iterated a 3D-printed lighting mount that blocks light leakage and diffuses output, resolving a reflection/flare issue identified during initial validation.
Installed and validated dual-illumination system (backlight and toplight modes) with external mode-switching, delivering an operator-ready characterization tool.

highlights: Developed a user-friendly prototype to collect patient data and evaluate a non-invasive method of measuring intracranial pressure via ultrasound in patients with acute neurologic injury.
Conducted end-user interviews comparing existing solutions to inform design decisions.
Designed and modeled bespoke soundproofing, heat recovery, and controlled climate enclosure systems in SolidWorks for corrugated cardboard production lines.
Built master assemblies of 1,000+ individual components nested within subassemblies and authored the engineering drawings for producing the dry-end enclosure parts.

experience.company: National Security Innovation Network, U.S. Air Force
highlights: Lead author of a cost-benefit analysis on stationing aerospace ground equipment at Gila Bend Auxiliary Airfield to prepare diverted F-35 aircraft for return to Luke Air Force Base.
Recommended strategy yielded a net annual saving of $20,700 (73%) and was implemented at Luke AFB with planned nationwide rollout.

experience.company: Greenaction for Health and Environmental Justice
highlights: Co-authored a report detailing how climate change processes including sea-level rise and groundwater rise will reactivate historically contaminated sites across the San Francisco Bay Area.

Primary researcher and author of the Bayview Hunters Point case study, covering decades of radiological and industrial contamination from the Hunters Point Naval Shipyard, the Tetra Tech data-falsification scandal, and ongoing environmental injustice.
Report adopted by Greenaction to advocate for government and press action on contamination cleanup before climate change worsens its effects on vulnerable communities.
Developed a black-powderless recovery system for the team's 9 km rocket, designing a motor-driven lead-screw mechanism to puncture a CO2 canister for parachute deployment.
Modeled descent characteristics under varying parachute parameters using MATLAB to size the drogue and main chutes.

experience.company: Mechanical Engineering Competition Team at Berkeley
highlights: Designed, proposed, and manufactured an EVA tool attachment mechanism for astronaut utility belts for NASA's Micro-g NExT Challenge.
Editor-in-Chief of the technical report; selected for Phase 2 and tested at NASA's Neutral Buoyancy Laboratory.

education.degree: M.S. Mechanical Engineering · Certificate of Engineering Leadership
education.details: Gordon Fellow, Gordon Institute of Engineering Leadership
GPA 3.89 / 4.0 · Cum Laude (Top 10%) · Dean's List, College of Engineering

coursework: Living Materials and their Biomaterial Replacements

publications.title: C60-based ionic salt electron shuttle for high-performance inverted perovskite solar modules
